title: Azure Logic Apps - DevOps, CI/CD, ARM Templates, And Environment Parameterization
applies_to: Deploying Logic Apps via ARM templates and GitHub Actions
Parameterizing Logic Apps across dev, staging, and production environments
Automating connector connection management in CI/CD pipelines

confirmed_facts.fact: Consumption Logic Apps: the workflow definition is embedded inside the ARM template under `properties.definition`. Export the current ARM template from the portal via Overview > Automation > Export template. The exported template includes the workflow JSON, parameters, and connection references.

Standard Logic Apps: the workflow definition lives in a `workflow.json` file inside the Logic App's file system (deployed like a Function App). The ARM template deploys the Logic App resource and app settings; the workflow JSON is deployed separately via zip deploy or the Azure Functions Core Tools. This separation enables independent versioning of infrastructure and workflow logic.

ARM template parameterization for Consumption Logic Apps: replace environment-specific values (SharePoint site URL, API base URLs, connection resource IDs) with ARM parameters: `"siteUrl": "[parameters('sharePointSiteUrl')]"`. Store parameter files per environment (`parameters.dev.json`, `parameters.prod.json`) in source control.

Logic Apps connections (managed connector connections) are separate ARM resources of type `Microsoft.Web/connections`. They must be created before the Logic App is deployed and referenced by resource ID. In CI/CD, create connections in a pre-deployment step or ensure they already exist in the target resource group.

GitHub Actions workflow for deploying a Consumption Logic App: use `azure/arm-deploy@v1` action. Example step: `- uses: azure/arm-deploy@v1 with: resourceGroupName: ${{ vars.RG_NAME }} template: ./logicapp.json parameters: ./parameters.${{ vars.ENV }}.json`. Authenticate using `azure/login@v1` with a service principal or OIDC federated identity.

GitHub Actions workflow for deploying a Standard Logic App workflow: use `azure/functions-action@v1` or `azure/webapps-deploy@v2` with the zip package. First zip the workflow folder: `zip -r workflow.zip .`, then deploy: `- uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v2 with: app-name: ${{ vars.LOGIC_APP_NAME }} package: workflow.zip`.

Standard Logic Apps support deployment slots (similar to Azure App Service). Use slots for blue-green deployments: deploy to a staging slot, smoke-test, then swap to production with `az logicapp deployment slot swap --name $appName --resource-group $rg --slot staging --target-slot production`. Slot swapping is near-instantaneous and preserves in-flight executions.

Use Azure CLI to disable a Logic App before deployment to prevent mid-deployment executions: `az logic workflow update --name $workflowName --resource-group $rg --state Disabled`. Re-enable after deployment: `--state Enabled`. For Standard, workflows within the app can be individually enabled/disabled.

implementation_notes: Store Logic App workflow definitions in Git alongside the pipeline scripts. For Consumption, export the ARM template after each portal change. For Standard, the workflow.json file is already a deployable artifact.

Use ARM template `parameters` for all values that differ between environments: API URLs, SharePoint site URLs, Key Vault names, connection resource IDs. Never hardcode environment-specific values in the workflow definition.
For managed connector connections (SharePoint, Outlook), document which connections must exist before deployment and include connection creation scripts in the CI/CD pipeline's bootstrap step.

Tag every deployed Logic App ARM resource with `environment`, `pipeline`, and `version` tags. This simplifies cost attribution and makes it easier to identify which deployment a failed run belongs to.
caveats: Managed connector connections contain OAuth credentials. They cannot be fully automated in CI/CD without interactive OAuth consent. Use Managed Identity-based connectors (HTTP with managed identity, built-in SharePoint in Standard) to avoid this limitation.
ARM template export from the portal may not include all parameterization. Review exported templates carefully and replace hardcoded resource IDs and URLs with parameters before committing.
Deployment slot swap for Standard Logic Apps swaps app settings (including connection strings) but not Key Vault references — ensure Key Vault access policies are configured for both slots.
